Workshops

Advertising Atelier​

The Advertising Workshop is a dynamic creative space where students transform theoretical knowledge into practical experience and gain direct exposure to the industry. Thanks to the department’s strong ties with the sector, students work on real briefs provided by actual brands and develop professional-level campaigns. Within the workshop, students establish their own simulation agencies and actively collaborate on both individual and group projects. Senior students mentor younger peers, while alumni share their experiences and support ongoing projects. This culture of collaboration prioritizes sharing and mutual growth over competition. Through regular workshop talks and events, students have the opportunity to interact directly with industry professionals and alumni. These events help students expand their knowledge of the field and build valuable professional networks. As a result, students experience the pace and expectations of the advertising industry throughout their studies, gaining access to internship and job opportunities even before graduation. The Advertising Workshop is a space where ideas are shared, creative projects take shape, and advertising is not just learned it is lived.

New Media Lab​

New Media Lab is an open learning environment where students can produce digital content, collaborate, and engage in social interaction. It is not just a classroom where courses are taught, but a creative space where students share ideas, work on digital projects, and develop content together. Lab remains accessible throughout the day, allowing students to use the computers for academic or personal projects, brainstorm with peers, or simply spend time in a productive atmosphere. Professionals working in areas such as new media, digital marketing, and content creation are frequently invited to the lab, providing students with the opportunity to engage directly with the industry and gain up-to-date insights and inspiration. Lab is equipped with the necessary technical infrastructure to support digital production workflows. Its flexible layout and comfortable seating areas make it suitable for both individual and group work. New Media Lab is more than just a workspace it is a collaborative hub that fosters creativity, encourages sharing, and supports students in actively engaging with the world of digital media.

Design Lab - VCD Workshop

Our creative design laboratories, DesignLab and VCD Workshop, are used for theoretical and practical courses where the topics covered in the courses are transformed into projects. Our laboratories provide students with a wide technical infrastructure including Apple computers with Adobe CC, design and animation software; graphic tablets, printer, scanner, sound system and projector. Our creative design laboratories are used 24/7, providing space for students to realize their individual works as well as projects for applied courses covering Interface Design, Interaction Design, Motion Graphic Design, Three Dimensional Digital Design, Mobile Application Design, Service Design, Web Design, Data Visualization, Game and Cyber Game Design, Video Production, Typography, Photography, Illustration and Animation. In addition to digital productions, DesignLab and VCD Workshop also provide opportunities for projects that require physical output such as models and prototypes thanks to their large working areas. In addition, our laboratories are actively used for extracurricular activities and workshops attended by professionals and academics from the industry.

Radio, Television and Cinema Studio

RTS Studio is a production space equipped with rich technical infrastructure, where students of the Radio, Television and Cinema Department receive hands-on training. Located within the Media Lab, the studio is available to students for both coursework and individual projects. Starting from their first year, students have the opportunity to gain practical experience in key production processes such as camera operation, lighting techniques, sound design, green screen applications, and scriptwriting. The equipment in the studio is regularly updated to meet current industry standards. All equipment in the RTS Studio is available for students to use free of charge through a reservation system. In addition, the studio includes an editing room equipped with iMac computers running Adobe software, allowing students to carry out editing and post-production work. This space is accessible 24/7 for both class assignments and personal projects. The studio also houses a soundproof podcast recording room, offering a professional environment for students to produce radio programs, podcast projects, and sound design work. RTS Studio not only provides technical resources, but also serves as a creative space that supports production, encourages critical thinking, and helps students gain valuable industry experience.

PR Atelier​

The Department of Public Relations and Information provides students with a study area where they can spend their extracurricular time to prepare themselves for their future professions within the framework of lectures or real customer relations representatives. Here, they conceptualize, develop, discuss and present the productions related to the courses they will choose to specialize in one of the political communication and corporate communication paths they will determine with a choice made, especially during the transition to third grade. In this way, they have a portfolio consisting of various projects that will help them a lot when entering the sector, participating in communication competitions in their fields, meeting with real customers, and having the opportunity to experience what they will bring to professional life when they are students.
